title: phoradendron serotinum subsp. macrophyllum
intro: Phoradendron serotinum subsp. macrophyllum, commonly known as the large-leafed mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of deciduous trees, primarily oak and pine. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Phoradendron serotinum subsp. macrophyllum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving respiratory function, among others. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
